mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent below-target inflation (CPIAUCSL < 230) signals accommodative central bank policy, which supports equity valuations through lower real interest rates and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL remains below 230 for 3+ months but 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO > 99.5) and payroll employment (PAYEMS > 135,000) signals robust supply-side capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO and PAYEMS both rise but 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 850) and real sales (RSAFS > 420,000) indicate resilient consumer demand, which drives equity market performance through increased corporate revenue visibility over 20 days.
failure_condition: HOUST and RSAFS both rise but 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 35% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 99.5) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings and economic growth expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases but 60-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE < 7.0) signals tightening labor markets, which drives equity returns through higher corporate profits and consumer spending over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE falls below 7.0 but 5-day SPX returns are negative in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate monetary easing amid disinflation, creating a favorable environment for equity markets through lower borrowing costs and improved risk appetite over 20 days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL both decline but 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 35% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1 > 2.5% annualized) reflects robust aggregate demand, which drives S&P 500 returns through increased corporate revenues and economic optimism over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 exceeds 2.5% but 60-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of cases.
